insights: The 'Cost Optimization' goal category is entirely absent from the dataset, confirming that no such goals exist in the current operational records.
All observed goal categories are limited to Database, Hardware, Inquiry / Help, Software, and Network — none of which map to cost-related objectives.
The short_description field contains only a single static value ('There was an issue'), eliminating any possibility of inferring cost optimization intent from textual content.
No partial matches for cost-, budget-, spend-, or optimization-related terms were found across any column, ruling out latent or inconsistently labeled instances.
summary: The investigation reveals that 'Cost Optimization' goals do not exist in the dataset — contradicting the core assumption underlying the user's goal. This absence means there are no success rates to analyze for this category, rendering the requested comparison and efficiency enhancement inapplicable. The dataset reflects only five operational goal types (Database, Hardware, Inquiry / Help, Software, Network), with no evidence of financial or resource-efficiency objectives. Next steps should focus on validating whether goal categorization logic, data ingestion pipelines, or upstream tagging processes are omitting or misclassifying cost-related goals.
